bibliographic_data.title: Generalized excitation of atomic multipole transitions by twisted light modes
bibliographic_data.abstract: A theoretical study is performed for the excitation of a single atom localized in the center of twisted light modes. Here we present the explicit dependence of excitation rates on critical parameters, such as the polarization of light, its orbital angular momentum projection, and the orientation of its propagation axis with respect to the atomic quantization axis. The effect of a spatial spread of the atom is also considered in detail. The expressions for transition rates obtained in this work can be used for any atom of arbitrary electronic configuration.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study provides explicit dependence of excitation rates on critical parameters such as the polarization and orientation of twisted light with respect to atomic quantization axis, for any atom.
core_content.key_methodology: Theoretical analysis of the excitation process driven by a light field propagating at different angles relative to the magnetic field. The authors derive general expressions for transition rates using multipole expansion of vector potential.
outcomes: Explicit dependence of excitation rates on critical parameters
General expressions for transition rates applicable to any atom
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper presents a theoretical study on the excitation of atomic multipole transitions by twisted light modes. The authors derive explicit dependencies of excitation rates on key parameters such as the polarization and orientation of the light with respect to the atomic quantization axis. They consider both perfectly localized atoms and those with finite spatial spread, providing general expressions for transition rates applicable to any atom or ion. These results are particularly relevant for understanding and optimizing experiments involving electric-dipole-forbidden transitions in optical atomic clocks.
